data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Stockpiles Post Sixth Straight Weekly Build
data.description:
By Anthony Harrup

U.S. crude-oil inventories rose more than expected in a sixth consecutive weekly increase, while gasoline and distillate stocks fell, according to data released Wednesday by the U.S. Energy Information Administration.

Commercial crude-oil stocks excluding the Strategic Petroleum Reserve rose by 5.5 million barrels to 461.6 million barrels in the week ended March 27 and were 0.1% above the five-year average for the time of year, the EIA said. Crude stocks were expected to have risen by 800,000 barrels, according to a Wall Street Journal survey of analysts.

Oil stored in the SPR fell by 378,000 barrels to 415.1 million barrels as the Department of Energy began releasing oil from the reserve in response to global supply disruptions in the Persian Gulf. Oil stocks at Cushing, Okla., the Nymex delivery hub, were up by 520,000 barrels at 31.5 million barrels.

U.S. crude-oil production was unchanged at 13.7 million barrels a day, imports were steady at 6.5 million barrels a day, and exports rose by 199,000 barrels a day to 3.5 million barrels a day.

Refineries ran at 92.1% of capacity, down from 92.9% the previous week, with crude input to refineries falling by 220,000 barrels a day to 16.4 million barrels a day. Refinery runs were forecast to have risen by half a percentage point in the Journal survey.

Gasoline inventories decreased by 586,000 barrels to 240.9 million barrels and were 4% above the five-year average, the EIA said. Gasoline demand fell by 238,000 barrels a day to 8.7 million barrels a day. Gasoline stocks were expected to have fallen by 1.5 million barrels.

Distillate fuel stocks were down by 2.1 million barrels at 117.8 million barrels, or 3% below the five-year average, against expectations of a 300,000-barrel decline. Distillate fuel demand rose by 471,000 barrels a day to 4 million barrels a day.

 
Change in U.S. oil inventories for the week ended March 27: 
 
                   Crude       Gasoline      Distillates        Refinery Use 
EIA data:           5.5          -0.6           -2.1                -0.8 
Forecast:           0.8          -1.5           -0.3                 0.5

Note: Numbers in millions of barrels, with the exception of refinery use, which is in percentage points.

Write to Anthony Harrup at anthony.harrup@wsj.com

data.title: XWELL 10-K: Revenue $29.21M, Continued Net Losses
data.description:
XWELL reported total net revenue of $29.210 million for the year, down 14% from the prior year, and continues to show accumulated deficits with an accumulated deficit of $489.697 million. The filing highlights margin pressure from reduced volumes and ongoing net losses while signaling steps taken to address liquidity and cost structure. Management notes actions to right-size operations and completed financing transactions to resolve going-concern doubt and fund near-term operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue, net: $29.210 million, decreased $4.687 million (14%) versus 2024 driven by lower XpresTest volumes.
  • Total cost of sales: $21.704 million, decreased $3.274 million (13%) versus 2024 reflecting closures and lower location labor/occupancy.
  • Gross Profit: $7.506 million (reported as derived from revenue less cost of sales), with margin pressure from reduced volumes.
  • General and administrative expense: $16.000 million, decreased $4.082 million (20%) year over year, reflecting improved efficiency.
  • Net income / (loss): Accumulated deficit of $489.697 million, indicating continued net losses (no single-line net income figure provided).
  • Net income per share: Not provided explicitly in Item 7/Item 8 disclosures.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue trend and drivers: Overall revenue declined 14% year over year to $29.2 million, primarily due to lower volumes from the XpresTest channel, partially offset by modest growth from Naples Wax service revenue.
  • Channel and portfolio adjustments: Company is right-sizing its airport footprint by closing underperforming locations, including Treat and three XpresSpa sites, to improve margins and reduce operating drag.
  • Brand and segment performance: Naples Wax expansion contributed to service revenue growth, though goodwill impairment indicates some assets underperformed relative to expectations.
  • Operational cost actions: Management reduced overhead and occupancy costs, achieving approximately a 20% reduction in G&A to improve cash burn.
  • Liquidity and strategic actions: Completed a Series H private placement and related repurchase transactions aimed at resolving going-concern doubt and providing funding for operations.

Original SEC Filing: XWELL, Inc. [ XWEL ] - 10-K - Apr. 01, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Kia to sell lower-priced electric vehicle in US
data.description:
By David Shepardson and Kalea Hall

Kia said Wednesday it will begin selling a lower-priced electric vehicle in the United States later this year as automakers work to recharge EV sales.

The Korean automaker said at the New York Auto Show it will offer the EV3 in the U.S. market starting later this year. Automakers are facing a tougher EV market in the United States after Congress repealed the $7,500 EV tax credit last year but higher gasoline prices in recent weeks prompted new interest in the EVs.

"The market is going to come back for EVs — maybe not as quickly as we all would have liked," said Russell Wager, vice president of marketing at Kia America. "We're committed to it."

Kia said the US EV market could return to where it was in the next three or four years.

David Christ, general manager of the Toyota Division at Toyota Motor North America , noted the Japanese automaker is introducing three EVs in the U.S. this year, and said higher fuel prices will give EVs a boost but unclear how much.

"I don't think they're going to get a boost back to the incentivized government money levels, but it'll be higher than it would have been without the gas price shock," Christ said.

The Alliance for Automotive Innovation, a trade group representing General Motors, Ford, Toyota Motor, ⁠Volkswagen, ​Hyundai, Stellantis and other major automakers, said EV sales were 9.6% of all U.S. sales in 2025 but fell to 6.5% in the last three months - the lowest since early 2022 - after the $7,500 EV tax credit expired on Sept. 30.

EVs are now 2.5% of total light-duty vehicles in operation in the United States. EV sales were 10.2% of all vehicles in 2024.

President Donald Trump has taken a series of steps to disincentivize EV purchases and production and make it easier to produce gas-powered models.

data.title: nCino Earnings, Guidance Could Lead to Multiple Re-Rating — Market Talk
data.description: nCino's strong 4Q earnings and solid guidance increases the potential for a re-rating of the stock's multiple, say Raymond James analysts. The company recorded larger than expected annual contract value bookings and offered a strong profitability outlook. nCino also landed record international bookings, while its platform pricing has led to improved annual contract value. "We see NCNO continuing to take a conservative approach to its guidance philosophy providing a positive backdrop for numbers over the coming quarters and a favorable risk/reward set up," the analysts say. (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Radnostix, Inc. Reports FY2025 Results - Second-Highest Annual Revenue in Company History; Delivers Strong Growth in Calibration & Reference Products and Advances Key Product Launches in 2025
data.description:
IDAHO FALLS, Idaho, April 1, 2026 /PRNewswire/ — Radnostix, Inc. (formerly International Isotopes Inc.) ("Radnostix" or the "Company"), a leading manufacturer of radioisotope-focused technologies for medical, industrial, and research applications, today announced its fiscal year 2025 financial results and corporate updates.

Fiscal 2025 Highlights

  • Total revenue reached $13.07 million, the second-highest annual revenue in Company history, reflecting healthy demand across core segments despite targeted supply chain challenges.
  • Calibration & Reference Products segment achieved record growth, with sales increasing 21% to $4.25 million, driven by new PET-focused products, expanded international sales, and recovery from prior-year Co-57 isotope shortages.
  • Theranostics Products segment generated $6.84 million in revenue, second-highest ever for the segment, despite multiple isotope shortages throughout the year. Our generic Sodium Iodide I-131 remains the only U.S.-manufactured generic of its kind.
  • Cobalt Products segment delivered $1.75 million in revenue while successfully completing a major processing hot cell refurbishment that is expected to extend asset life by 15--20 years and reduce annual radiological waste costs by approximately $150,000. We are the only U.S.-based manufacturer of finished Cobalt-60 products.
  • Medical Devices segment showed strong momentum with $229,000 in revenue from third-party products and key progress toward 2026 launches of the EasyFill Automated Capsule System and RadVent products.
  • Positive operating cash flow of $620,000, underscoring disciplined cost management and operational efficiency despite raw material challenges.
  • Completed construction and received initial NRC license amendment for a dedicated Calibration & Reference Products manufacturing facility, positioning the Company for significant capacity expansion and product portfolio growth in 2026.
  • Executed mutual termination of the DUF6 asset sale agreement; Radnostix regained full control of these strategic assets amid the global nuclear energy boom and will evaluate development options, including potential license amendments for expanded uranium-related activities.

"2025 was a year of refinement for Radnostix, where we focused on internal improvements to, which we believe will set us up for long-term success. Despite isotope shortages and management decision to delay Q4 cobalt-60 projects to 2026, we delivered the second-highest revenue in our history while making substantial investments in our future. We completed critical facility upgrades, advancing our Medical Devices pipeline, and strengthening our position as a trusted domestic supplier of high-value radioisotope products." Said Shahe Bagerdjian, President and Chief Executive Officer, adding "Our Calibration & Reference Products segment posted healthy growth, and we successfully refurbished our cobalt-60 hot cells, securing long-term production capability and cost savings. The name change to Radnostix implemented in Q4 of 2025 reflects our sharpened focus on innovation in advanced radioisotope technologies."

"Looking ahead, we are excited about the anticipated 2026 commercial launches of EasyFill and RadVent, expanded international opportunities, and the strategic value of our retained DUF6 assets in today's booming nuclear market."

2026 Outlook

The Company enters 2026 with strong momentum and several key catalysts:

  • Full commercial launch of the EasyFill Automated Capsule System (targeted Q3 2026) and RadVent products.
  • Continued expansion of the Calibration & Reference Products facility and product line.
  • Submitting a theranostics GMP API drug master file.
  • Strategic evaluation of the DUF6 assets to capitalize on renewed global interest in uranium fuel cycle services. Twelve months ended - December 31 2025 2024 $ Change % Change ------------ ------------ ---------- --------

About Radnostix (formerly International Isotopes Inc.): Radnostix, Inc. (formerly International Isotopes Inc.) (INIS), established in 1995, with its headquarters in Idaho Falls, ID, USA, manufactures a wide range of radioisotope-focused products. Radnostix manufactures and supplies generic sodium iodide I-131 radio-pharmaceutical drug product for hyperthyroidism and thyroid cancer. Radnostix provides contract manufacturing of various drug products as well as radioisotope API supply for 3rd party theranostics clients. Radnostix manufactures and distributes a complete line of calibration and reference standards for nuclear pharmacies and SPECT/PET imaging systems as well as industrial calibration standards under its RadQual brand. For more information, visit www.radnostix.com.

Non-GAAP Financial Measures

This report contains financial measures that do not comply with U.S. generally accepted accounting principles ("GAAP"), such as EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA. EBITDA is defined as net income plus interest, income taxes, depreciation and amortization. Adjusted EBITDA is defined as EBITDA, adjusted to exclude items that are deemed to be unusual and non-recurring, and that we do not believe are indicative of the companies recurring operating performance, such as non-cash stock-based compensation, gain on disposal of assets, and costs associated with NRC enforcement matters and our medical devices buildout.

These non-GAAP financial measures are supplemental measures to our results of operations as reported under GAAP. Our management uses these measures to better analyze our financial results and business operations. In management's opinion, these non-GAAP measures are useful to investors and other users of our financial statements by providing greater transparency into the ongoing operating performance of the Company and its future outlook. Such measures should not be considered alternatives to net income or any other performance measures derived in accordance with GAAP. The Company's measurement of EBITDA and Adjusted EBITDA may not be comparable to similar measures of other companies as they are not performance measures calculated in accordance with GAAP.

See the tables above for reconciliations of GAAP to non-GAAP measures.

Information contained in such forward-looking statements is based on current expectations and is subject to change. data.title: Russian industrial activity shrinks in March, highlighting deeper problems
data.description:
Key points:
  • PMI shows manufacturing contracting at fastest pace in 2026 in March
  • Analysts, government are revising growth forecasts
  • Central bank pointed to a surprise weakening of demand
  • Aluminium billionaire calls on Russians to work more

By Gleb Bryanski

Russia's manufacturing sector contracted at the sharpest pace this year in March, an S&P Global survey showed on Wednesday, highlighting the deeper problems faced by the $2.6 trillion economy despite an expected oil sales windfall amid conflict in the Middle East.

The economy, hit by high interest rates and Western sanctions, slowed to 1% growth last year from 4.9% the year before. Low oil prices at the start of this year exacerbated the situation, and the economy contracted 2.1% in January.

The S&P Global Russia Manufacturing Purchasing Managers' Index fell to 48.3 in March from 49.5 in February, sinking further below the 50 mark separating growth from contraction.

"The degree of optimism was the lowest in almost four years and well below the series average," S&P Global said in a statement, adding that concerns regarding customer solvency and subdued demand weighed on companies' expectations.

Russian industrial output contracted by 0.8% in January and by 0.9% in February, and based on the S&P Global survey it could decline further in March, raising the risks of an economic contraction in the first half of 2026.

RISKS OF ECONOMY CONTRACTING IN FIRST HALF OF 2026

The government is set to cut its growth forecast down from the optimistic projection of 1.3% with the Economy Minister Maxim Reshetnikov saying that even faster reductions in the central bank's benchmark interest rate will not help boost growth this year.

A Reuters poll of 16 economists saw the economy growing by 0.8% this year, down from a forecast of 1% a month ago, and some highlighted the risks of the economy contracting in the first half.

S&P Global said that export demand weakened for a fifth consecutive month in March, though the pace of decline softened slightly. Survey respondents cited the war in the Middle East and customer uncertainty as factors weighing on external sales.

The central bank said in the minutes of its March 20 rate-setting meeting published on Wednesday that "economic activity at the beginning of 2026 has been more subdued than it expected in February."

Russia's TsMAKP economic think tank - which works with the government - and the central bank in its minutes both noted an unexpected sharp fall in consumer demand at the start of 2026.

"The downward trend is largely driven by a combination of tight monetary policy and the strengthening of the rouble, and can be seen across all major demand components: investment, household consumption, and exports," TsMAKP analysts said.

BILLIONAIRE TELLS RUSSIANS TO WORK MORE

Intensified Ukrainian drone attacks, which knocked out 40% of Russia's oil export capacity and at least 5% of its fertilizer industry, are having an increased impact on industry, gradually expanding from energy to other sectors.

Several major Russian companies in vulnerable and indebted sectors such as steelmaking and coal mining, have announced job cuts, a move that will cool Russia's overheated labour market.

"Employment fell for the fourth month running, albeit at the softest pace in 2026 so far. The decrease was attributed to redundancies following lower output levels and moves to piecework pay," S&P Global said.

In a statement that has stirred public debate, sanctioned aluminium billionaire Oleg Deripaska proposed a 12-hour, six-day working week as a remedy to Russia's economic woes.

"We do not have many resources. In fact, we have only one, and it is rooted in a national characteristic: In difficult moments, we know how to rally and work harder," the businessman wrote.

data.title: U.S. manufacturers see best month in 2 1/2 years, but Iran war threatens to derail progress
data.description:
By Jeffry Bartash

ISM survey hits highest level since mid-2022

Auto workers assemble new trucks.

Check back for updates.

American manufacturers grew in March at the fastest pace in two and a half years, by one measure, but the conflict with Iran added a new level of uncertainty just as the effects of the Trump tariffs were fading.

The Institute for Supply Management's survey of manufacturers was inched up to 52.7 in March from 52.6% in the prior month. It was the highest reading since August 2022.

Any number above 50% indicates business is growing.

The ISM surveys executives every month about how their businesses are doing. Manufacturers no longer play an outsized role in the U.S. economy, but they still employ about 13 million people.

Market reaction: The Dow Jones Industrial Average DJIA and S&P 500 SPX rose in Wednesday trading.

-Jeffry Bartash

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: ISM Manufacturing Survey Shows Rising Price Pressures
data.description:
By Matt Grossman

Factory activity expanded in the U.S. in March, but the latest monthly reading from the Institute for Supply Management flashed a strong warning that inflation is resurgent amid the war in Iran.

The ISM's purchasing managers' index was 52.7 last month, versus 52.4 in February. Readings above 50 indicate a sectoral expansion. Analysts polled by The Wall Street Journal were expecting a reading of 52.1.

The survey's prices index hit 78.3, the highest reading since 2022. It was a jump from 70.5 in February.

All the commodities categories tracked by the survey rose in price, and none declined. Respondents to the survey said the war is adding to uncertainty and raising costs.

"Current Middle East unrest is already starting to impact business operations by increasing lead times, costs, container delays and the like," one manager in the food and beverage category told the survey.

On the other hand, some respondents told the survey that the Supreme Court's decision to strike down much of President Trump's tariff agenda could provide a lift.

At 48.7, the index for manufacturing employment indicated that jobs in the sector remained in contraction. ADP's private-sector payrolls report for March, released earlier Wednesday morning, also showed that manufacturing jobs fell last month.

Write to Matt Grossman at matt.grossman@wsj.com

data.title: Mark Cohen Named #1 Mortgage Broker, #1 Non-QM Originator In the U.S.
data.description:
Scotsman Guide 2026 Rankings Recognize Cohen Financial Group Founder for the Fourth Consecutive Year as Top Broker - Closes $933M in Loans in 2025

BEVERLY HILLS, CA / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / Mark Cohen, Founder and CEO of Cohen Financial Group, has once again been named the #1 Mortgage Broker in the United States and the #1 Non-QM Originator in the United States by Scotsman Guide, the mortgage industry's most respected and widely followed ranking publication in its 2026 rankings released today, April 1, 2026. This marks Cohen's fourth consecutive year as the nation's #1 Mortgage Broker and his fifth consecutive year as the nation's #1 Non-QM Originator.

In 2025, Cohen closed $933,705,193 in loan volume across 692 loans, building on $790 million in 2024, $743 million in 2023, and $751 million in 2022. These figures reflect not only Cohen's extraordinary production capacity, but the depth of trust that borrowers, real estate professionals, and lenders place in him year after year. With a career spanning nearly four decades, Cohen has now originated over 31,000 loans totaling more than $18 billion in funded volume - and remains the only mortgage broker in the United States to average $1 billion in annual loan originations for more than a decade.

"To receive this recognition from Scotsman Guide for the fourth consecutive year as the #1 Mortgage Broker and the fifth consecutive year as the #1 Non-QM Originator in the country is humbling. This is a testament to the relentless dedication of my team and the unwavering loyalty of our clients. We show up every day committed to excellence, and these rankings reflect that commitment."

- Mark Cohen, Founder & CEO, Cohen Financial Group

Cohen's recognition as the #1 Non-QM Originator for the fifth straight year underscores his unmatched expertise in the Non-Qualified Mortgage space. Non-QM lending has become an essential pathway for self-employed borrowers, real estate investors, foreign nationals, and high-net-worth individuals who require flexible underwriting solutions outside of conventional agency guidelines. Cohen Financial Group's mastery of bank statement loans, DSCR loans, asset depletion loans, and other Non-QM products has positioned the firm as the clear leader in this rapidly growing and complex segment of the market.

Based in Los Angeles and serving clients throughout California and across the United States, Cohen Financial Group has built its reputation on the ability to close complex loans when others cannot. With over 16 consecutive years ranked in the top 1% of Scotsman Guide's national rankings, Cohen is widely regarded as the go-to mortgage broker for luxury residential purchases, jumbo financing, commercial real estate, and high-profile Non-QM transactions. More than 76% of Cohen's business comes from repeat clients and referrals - a reflection of the lasting relationships he builds and the results he consistently delivers.

As the top-ranked mortgage broker and Non-QM originator in America, Mark Cohen and Cohen Financial Group enter 2026 with continued momentum, an expanded lending network, and the same singular focus that has defined the firm since its founding: getting clients to the closing table with the best possible terms.

ABOUT COHEN FINANCIAL GROUP

Cohen Financial Group is headquartered in Beverly Hills, California, founded by Mark Cohen in 1986. Specializing in residential, commercial, jumbo, refinance, and Non-Qualified Mortgage (Non-QM) lending, Cohen Financial Group serves a diverse clientele ranging from first-time homebuyers to seasoned real estate investors and ultra-high-net-worth individuals. Mark Cohen has been ranked #1 Mortgage Broker in the United States by Scotsman Guide for four consecutive years (2023-2026) and #1 Non-QM Originator for five consecutive years (2022-2026). For more information, visit www.cohenfinancialgroup.com.

MEDIA CONTACT

Sarah Crouse

Draft Creative Group

Sarah@draftcreativegroup.com

draftcreativegroup.com

SOURCE: Cohen Financial Group

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: Broker tips: Future, Berkeley, Barratt Redrow
data.description:
Following Future's first half pre-close statement on 31 March, Berenberg cut its FY26-28 earnings per share estimates by roughly 20-30%, noting that over the coming years, it expects Future's programmatic and e-commerce revenue to continue to be negatively affected by audience declines as large language models scale.

"These are high-margin revenue streams and, although Future is looking to offset these declines through other strategic initiatives, these initiatives are lower margin," said Berenberg. "This is likely to result in a continued adverse margin mix shift, which is reflected in our FY26-28 forecasts."

The German bank noted that the market "remains highly sceptical" about businesses directly impacted by AI and, although its valuation "appears cheap", does not expect a material re-rating from Future until it sees evidence of the impact of AI easing.

"Our new price target of 330p is based on a sum-of-the-parts and peer multiple of 3.7x applied

to our FY28 EPS estimate. We expect greater focus on realising shareholder value from the portfolio given the valuation, with this being the main risk to our 'hold' thesis in the short term," said Berenberg.

"We also assume margin declines continue as revenue from the company's strategic initiatives grows, which is lower margin than programmatic and e-commerce. This results in a 29% and 31% cut to our FY27 and FY28 EPS estimates, respectively."

RBC Capital Markets upgraded Berkeley and Barratt Redrow on Wednesday as it took a look at UK housebuilders.

The Canadian bank said that besides 'Help to Buy', it was difficult to see what will drive housebuilder share prices higher in the near term.

"Asset valuations are back to levels last seen in the Great Financial Crisis and without a powerful catalyst we don't see how to get valuations 'back to the future'," it said. "The war in Iran has turned newsflow negative lengthening the handle of the hockey stick of recovery. The value proposition is clear, but until newsflow turns positive there is limited fear of missing out."

RBC said it continues to believe Persimmon will outperform, Taylor Wimpey will reward the patient and Crest Nicholson will continue its turn around. All three were kept at 'outperform'. Persimmon's price target was cut to 1,350p from 1,750p, while Taylor Wimpey's was cut to 120p from 155p and Crest Nicholson was reduced to 155p from 215p.

RBC upgraded Barratt Redrow to 'outperform' from 'sector perform' on valuation grounds and lifted its price target on the stock to 350p from 425p, and upgraded Berkeley to 'sector perform' from 'underperform' due to the "robustness" of its business model, keeping the price target at 3,900p. However, the bank said it sees "darker days ahead" for Vistry, which it kept at 'underperform' and slashed its price target to 180p from 385p.

data.title: ServiceNow to Announce First Quarter 2026 Financial Results on April 22
data.description:
SANTA CLARA, Calif.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 01, 2026--

ServiceNow today announced that it will release financial results for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, following the close of market on Wednesday, April 22, 2026. ServiceNow will host a conference call and live webcast to discuss the financial results.

Conference Call Details

The conference call will begin at 2 p.m. Pacific Time (21:00 GMT) on April 22, 2026. Interested parties may listen to the call by dialing (888) 330-2455 (Passcode: 8135305), or if outside North America, by dialing (240) 789-2717 (Passcode: 8135305). Individuals may access the live teleconference from this webcast (https://events.q4inc.com/attendee/481376230).

An audio replay of the conference call and webcast will be available two hours after its completion and will be accessible for 30 days. To hear the replay, interested parties may go to the investor relations section of the ServiceNow website or dial 800-770-2030 (Passcode: 8135305), or if outside North America, by dialing 647-362-9199 (Passcode: 8135305).

About ServiceNow

ServiceNow is the AI control tower for business reinvention. The ServiceNow AI Platform integrates with any cloud, any model, and any data source to orchestrate how work flows across the enterprise. By unifying legacy systems, departmental tools, cloud applications, and AI agents, ServiceNow provides a single pane of glass that connects intelligence to execution across every corner of business. With more than 80 billion workflows running on the platform each year, ServiceNow helps organizations turn fragmented operations into coordinated, autonomous workflows that deliver measurable results. Learn how ServiceNow puts AI to work for people at www.servicenow.com.

data.title: European Banks Avoid Issuing High-Risk Bonds in March Given Increased Volatility — Market Talk
data.description: European banks skipped issuing new subordinated bonds in March due to high spread volatility in the segment, ING's Marine Leleux says in a note. Subordinated bonds are considered risky investments due to their low ranking in repayment priority. Investors demand a higher risk premium for investing in subordinated bonds than they do on senior bank bonds which have a higher ranking in repayment priority. "While we note some senior unsecured issuances (2.75 billion euros [$3.2 billion] in senior preferred and 3 billion euros in senior bail-in bonds), no subordinated bonds were issued over March," Leleux says. (miriam.mukuru@wsj.com)

data.title: Sustained High Fuel Prices Could Lead to Lower Demand in U.S. and Emerging Markets, Goldman Sachs Says — OPIS
data.description:
An extended global oil shortage driven by the continuing Middle East conflict could lead to sharp price-driven reductions in U.S. gasoline and diesel demand and lower jet fuel and Asian petrochemical consumption, Goldman Sachs energy analysts said on Tuesday.

The bank said that since Feb. 1, the global weighted average retail price for gasoline has risen by 27% while the average price for diesel has increased by 31% for diesel.

In the Americas, average gasoline and diesel prices rose by 34% and 43%, respectively, over the last two months. The average gasoline price in Europe rose by 11% over the same period and the average diesel price increased by 23%.

Both gasoline and diesel price increases have varied across countries, particularly in emerging economies, Goldman added.

While government policy has kept retail fuel prices unchanged in India since February, diesel prices have risen by nearly 150% in South Africa and by about 115% in the Philippines, the analysts said.

"If Middle East oil exports were to remain low for longer, we see potential for significant price-driven reductions in demand for gasoline and diesel in the largest markets with flexible prices like the U.S., and in emerging markets where prices have risen sharply and fuel demand is likely relatively price sensitive" like South Africa, Philippines, Malaysia, Vietnam, the bank said.

Goldman said price-driven declines in demand have been modest given the Mideast supply losses, with gasoline demand down slightly in the U.S. and China.

The bank, however, said demand destruction from higher prices has been seen in certain sectors, including in global aviation and Asian petrochemicals.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeffrey Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com

data.title: Redmond HVAC Contractor Earns Triple Recognition in 60 Days, Reflecting a Shift in How Homeowners Vet Contractors
data.description:
SEATTLE, WA / ACCESS Newswire / April 1, 2026 / In roughly two months, three independent organizations that evaluate home service contractors each reached the same conclusion: Home Comfort Alliance, an HVAC contractor serving the Greater Puget Sound area, is among the most reliable in its market. The coincidence of timing has turned what might otherwise be routine business recognition into something harder to dismiss.

Between January and February 2026, Home Comfort Alliance received three distinct recognitions: a Top 10 HVAC Contractor designation from Trust Analytica, Certified Best Pick status from Best Pick Reports, and the Best HVAC Contractor in Redmond, WA title from Business Rate. Each organization uses its own methodology, ranging from verified customer review audits and analysis of complaint history to licensing checks and insurance verification.

The recognitions themselves aren't the story; what they signal is. Homeowners today don't just ask friends for referrals; they turn to third-party platforms to help them navigate an industry that feels saturated with choices. When multiple platforms, each vetting contractors by their own standards, independently land on the same company, that says something no single endorsement really can.

Home Comfort Alliance's Marketing Director framed it this way:

"These recognitions simply reflect what our customers already know about Home Comfort Alliance - expert technicians, reliable service, and a team that genuinely cares about their comfort. That's why they call us The Comfort People." - Timi Oyebanjo

What Each Organization Actually Measures The three organizations behind these recognitions operate independently and evaluate contractors through different lenses. Trust Analytica compiles its rankings from aggregated reputation data, customer satisfaction records, and reliability indicators across local markets. Its Top 10 list for Redmond HVAC contractors is a data-driven output, and the same organization separately named Home Comfort Alliance a Top 10 HVAC Contractor for Everett in 2025.Best Pick Reports layers in background checks and licensing verification, with an 85% recommendation rate from verified customers. Certification must be re-earned annually, and inclusion is by invitation only. Business Rate rounds out the picture by evaluating overall performance, customer experience, and community standing, with its Best HVAC Contractor designation showcasing sustained excellence in customer satisfaction and review performance over a period, rather than a single standout project.

Industry Credentials and Utility Partnerships

Beyond recognition, the company holds several credentials relevant to consumers in the region. All their technicians hold NATE certification, a nationally recognized standard for HVAC professionals that require demonstrated technical competence. The Better Business Bureau also accredits the company.

Home Comfort Alliance also holds trade ally status with Snohomish County PUD, Tacoma Public Utilities, Puget Sound Energy, and Seattle City Light, which allows the company to connect customers with energy-efficiency rebate programs. For homeowners navigating the cost of HVAC installations, those utility-linked rebates can be a meaningful factor in total project cost.

About Home Comfort Alliance

Home Comfort Alliance, headquartered in Redmond, is an HVAC contractor serving homeowners across the Greater Puget Sound area, including Bellevue, Kirkland, Seattle, Renton, Tacoma, Puyallup, Gig Harbor, Bremerton, and Olympia. The company provides heating, cooling, plumbing, and electrical services, and holds trade ally partnerships with multiple regional utility providers.

Media Contact

Timi Oyebanjo, Marketing Director

Home Comfort Alliance

Redmond, Washington

www.homecomfortalliance.com

toyebanjo@homecomfortalliance.com

SOURCE: Home Comfort Alliance

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Inventories Increase Much More Than Expected
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Crude oil inventories in the U.S. increased by much more than expected in the week ended March 27th, according to a report released by the Energy Information Administration on Wednesday.

The EIA said crude oil inventories climbed by 5.5 million barrels last week after jumping by 6.9 million barrels in the previous week. Economists had expected crude oil inventories to rise by 2.0 million barrels.

At 461.6 million barrels, U.S. crude oil inventories are about 0.1 percent above the five-year average for this time of year, the EIA said.

Meanwhile, the report said gasoline inventories dipped by 0.6 million barrels last week but remain 4 percent above the five-year average for this time of year.

Distillate fuel inventories, which include heating oil and diesel, also fell by 2.1 million barrels last week and are about 3 percent below the five-year average for this time of year, the EIA said.

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Trump Makes History at High Court, Attending Birthright Case. Why Markets Should Watch. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Emily Russell

President Donald Trump made history on Wednesday, sitting in on oral arguments in the birthright citizenship case at the Supreme Court.

Trump and Attorney General Pam Bondi are listening as justices consider Trump v. Barbara, a case challenging the president's executive order in January 2025 to restrict citizenship to children of a U.S. citizen or permanent U.S. resident. The order never went into effect.

The plaintiffs argue that the order violates Section 1 of the Constitution's 14th amendment, which says "all persons born or naturalized in the U.S., and subject to the jurisdiction thereof, are citizens of the U.S."

Presidents have historically stayed away from Supreme Court proceedings to avoid the appearance of interference with the judicial branch. But Trump has become increasingly vocal about his dissatisfaction with the judiciary in his second term.

As with Trump's attacks on the Federal Reserve chair, mounting attacks on the judiciary could shake investors' faith in rule of law in the U.S., which helps underpin the U.S. dollar's strength and helps hold down yields on U.S. government debt.

Trump said last month that the six justices who voted to strike down his use of the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to issue broad tariffs were "bad for our country" and "sicken me." That majority included two justices he appointed.

He has also called on congressional Republicans to pass "tough" legislation that "cracks down on rogue judges."

"We got rogue judges that are criminals," he said. "They're criminals, what they do to our country, the decisions that they hand down and hurt our country,"

His administration lost the majority of its cases in lower courts last year, but won a majority of its cases at the appellate courts and in the Supreme Court.

Write to emily.russell@barrons.com.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.
